content:
Quebec's defining advantage for tech companies is the ability to stack the provincial 37.5% R&D tax credit with the federal SR&ED program. This combination can subsidize well over 55% of your R&D payroll. If you are doing true innovation, it is often more profitable to maximize these guaranteed tax credits than to spend months chasing competitive, uncertain grants.

shortAnswer: Yes — Quebec small businesses can access Investissement Québec (IQ) modernization loans (ESSOR), Service Québec workforce training grants (MFOR up to 50% matching), and R&D tax credits up to 37.5%. These programs can be stacked with federal tools like IRAP and SR&ED to fund up to 75-85% of eligible engineering operations under ideal conditions.
shortAnswerQuestion: What small business grants and funding are available in Quebec for 2026?

faq.question: What is the Service Québec training grant limit?
faq.answer: The Mesures de formation (MFOR) program typically covers up to 50% of eligible training costs (such as instructor fees and courseware) to help upskill your staff.

faq.question: Are Investissement Québec ESSOR programs repayable?
faq.answer: ESSOR offers a combination of financial products. Modernization and feasibility grants are non-repayable, while larger commercialization scale-ups are structured as low-interest or interest-free loans.

faq.question: Can I stack Quebec tax credits with federal SR&ED?
faq.answer: Yes. Quebec provincial R&D tax credits are designed to stack directly on top of the federal Scientific Research and Experimental Development (SR&ED) program, allowing CCPCs to recover a substantial portion of eligible R&D expenditures.

expertTip.title: Bilingual Application Advantage
expertTip.content: Provincial applications for Investissement Québec and Service Québec must be submitted in French. Ensure all business plans and technical project descriptions are professionally translated to avoid evaluation delays.
